claims.claim: Modular Ethereum: rollups, restaking, shared sequencing
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Speculation-only crypto — 'the casino' (memecoins, leverage, airdrop farming, MEV)
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Neutral on trade / bullish on technology; under-weights token price, no price calls
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: New strong emphasis on cross-chain interoperability and universal cross-chain asset access (LayerZero podcast, lead investment in Universal — 'Circle for cryptoassets'), framed around composability rather than the prediction's framing of bridges mainly as a security concern
Embrace of secure-hardware/TEE-based privacy (Seismic investment) as an approach distinct from the prediction's pure-ZK framing of privacy
New explicit conviction that DeFi composability is a primary unlock (Universal, 'why composability matters')
Public 2025 prediction that formal verification will become increasingly important — a primitive not in the original prediction

Active, celebratory political/regulatory engagement (congratulating David Sacks, CFTC nominee, McHenry) — moving from abstract concern about hostility to optimism about a favorable US regulatory regime
New emphasis on abstraction/'software era' developer tooling for web3 (Halliday) and agentic AI calling smart contracts
notes: Window is dominated by deal announcements and ecosystem boosts that strongly confirm the builder/technology lens and the four core excitement sectors (AI×crypto via Gensyn RL swarm and 'decentralize AI = verification'; ZK/privacy via Aleo and Seismic; modular Ethereum via Espresso, EigenLayer/Infura, Syndicate; DePIN via Helium). Risk_regime confirmed by consistent technology-and-team framing plus 'not investment advice' disclaimers and zero price/macro calls. Stablecoins, the casino/memecoin concerns, SOL, and NEAR simply weren't addressed (absent, not contradicted). The regulatory axis is confirmed via engagement, though his tone shifted toward optimism as pro-crypto appointments materialized. No statements contradicted the prediction.
